cards.title: Mark Zuckerberg: AI Progress Slower Than Expected
cards.body: Meta's CEO told staff that AI development efforts are not advancing as quickly as anticipated. The admission comes as the company continues investing heavily in AI infrastructure and capabilities.

cards.title: Meta Quietly Launches Pocket Gaming App
cards.body: Meta released Pocket, an experimental AI app enabling users to generate and share interactive mini games via text prompts. The launch represents Meta's latest move into generative AI applications.

cards.title: Anthropic in Custom Chip Talks With Samsung
cards.body: Anthropic is discussing custom chip development with Samsung, following OpenAI's recent announcement of its own AI chip partnership with Broadcom. The move signals competitive infrastructure buildout among leading AI companies.

cards.title: OpenAI Proposes 5% Equity for Sovereign Wealth
cards.body: CEO Sam Altman proposed donating 5% of OpenAI's equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving public benefit sharing discussions. The proposal aims to let the public participate in AI industry gains.

cards.title: Microsoft Launches AI Deployment Company
cards.body: Microsoft committed $2.5 billion to a new AI deployment group, joining Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic in the infrastructure space. The move signals major cloud providers racing to capture AI infrastructure demand.

cards.title: Indian Tycoon Bets $30M on Office Alternative
cards.body: Bhavin Turakhia is investing $30 million into Neo, an AI-powered alternative to Microsoft Office and Google Workspace. The venture marks his fifth company and latest play in enterprise software.

cards.title: SpaceX Showed Investors AI Device Prototype
cards.body: SpaceX reportedly demonstrated a "handset-like" AI device to investors before going public. The move signals SpaceX's potential expansion into wireless and consumer AI hardware.

cards.title: Ashton Kutcher Launches New VC Infrastructure Fund
cards.body: Kutcher and Morgan Beller are launching a new venture fund focusing on AI infrastructure and energy rather than AI labs directly. The fund targets the foundational layer powering leading AI companies.

cards.title: Cloudflare Enforces AI Content Payment Policy
cards.body: Cloudflare requires AI companies to separate search and training crawlers by September 15 or face blocking on publisher sites. The policy pushes AI companies to pay for published content access.

cards.title: Venice AI Reaches Unicorn Status
cards.body: Privacy-focused Venice AI secured $65 million in Series A funding and achieved unicorn valuation status. The company is already profitable with over $70 million in annualized revenue.

cards.title: Google Launches Gemini Spark on Mac
cards.body: Google's 24/7 agentic assistant Gemini Spark is now available on Mac with expanded features. Updates include real-time tracking and support for additional applications.

cards.title: Meta Develops Cloud Compute Infrastructure Business
cards.body: Meta is planning to monetize excess AI compute by selling access through a cloud infrastructure business. The move positions Meta against AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

cards.title: Trump Administration Drops Anthropic Model Restrictions
cards.body: The Trump administration lifted restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models. The move reflects inconsistent AI policy approaches leaving companies uncertain about future regulations.

cards.title: Wayve Launches $85M Employee Tender Offer
cards.body: Wayve announced an $85 million employee tender offer valuing the company at $8.5 billion. The offering reflects a growing trend of AI startups using tenders for talent retention.

cards.title: OpenClaw Arrives on Android and iOS
cards.body: The free open-source agentic program OpenClaw is now available on mobile platforms. The release expands access to AI agents for smartphone users.

cards.title: Browser Wars Shift Beyond Search Competition
cards.body: Multiple alternative browsers are challenging Chrome and Safari dominance in the market. The shift reflects changing priorities in browser functionality and user preferences.

cards.title: Jersey Mike's IPO Highlights AI Hype Bubble
cards.body: Even sandwich shop Jersey Mike's mentions AI in IPO documents despite minimal relevance to operations. The trend exemplifies how pervasive AI hype has become across industries.

cards.title: Vinton Cerf Steps Down as Google Evangelist
cards.body: Internet protocol pioneer Vinton Cerf is retiring as Google's chief internet evangelist. His departure marks the end of an era for one of the internet's founding figures.

cards.title: OpenClaw Automates Dating Through Instagram Scripts
cards.body: Ben Guez created an automated dating script using OpenClaw and Claude code integrated with Instagram. The tool demonstrates unconventional AI applications in personal life.

cards.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Builders Stage Announced
cards.body: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature the Builders Stage for 10,000+ founders and investors. The event offers practical strategies for building and scaling successful companies with early registration discounts.

cards.title: Mark Zuckerberg: AI agents progressing slower than expected
cards.body: Meta's CEO told staff that AI development efforts are not moving as quickly as anticipated. The comments reveal potential delays in the company's AI roadmap.

cards.title: Meta launches Pocket, AI-powered gaming app
cards.body: Meta quietly released Pocket, an experimental AI app enabling users to generate and share interactive mini-games using text prompts. The app showcases advances in accessible AI tools.

cards.title: Anthropic discusses custom AI chip with Samsung
cards.body: Anthropic is negotiating a custom chip partnership with Samsung, following OpenAI's recent custom AI chip announcement. The move reflects growing competition in AI infrastructure.

cards.title: OpenAI proposes public equity stake in company
cards.body: CEO Sam Altman proposed donating 5% of OpenAI's equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, allowing public participation in AI company gains. The proposal reignites discussions about democratizing AI wealth.

cards.title: Microsoft launches $2.5B AI deployment business
cards.body: Microsoft established a dedicated AI deployment group with $2.5 billion commitment, competing with Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic. The move signals increased corporate focus on AI infrastructure scaling.

cards.title: Indian tech mogul invests $30M in AI Office alternative
cards.body: Bhavin Turakhia is funding Neo, an AI-powered alternative to Microsoft Office and Google Apps. The venture represents growing competition in enterprise AI software.

cards.title: SpaceX develops handset-like AI device prototype
cards.body: SpaceX demonstrated an AI device prototype resembling a phone to investors ahead of going public. The prototype signals potential expansion into wireless and AI hardware.

cards.title: Ashton Kutcher launches VC firm focusing on AI infrastructure
cards.body: Kutcher and Morgan Beller are launching a new VC fund targeting AI infrastructure and energy companies. The move reflects growing interest in AI ecosystem layer-building.

cards.title: Cloudflare enforces AI training data payment policy
cards.body: Cloudflare mandates AI companies separate search and training web crawlers by September 15 or face blocking. The policy aims to protect publisher content rights in AI development.

cards.title: Venice AI reaches unicorn status with $65M Series A
cards.body: Privacy-first AI platform Venice AI raised $65 million at $1 billion valuation while achieving profitability. The startup reports $70 million annualized revenue.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark agentic assistant reaches Mac
cards.body: Google expanded its 24/7 agentic assistant Gemini Spark to Mac with real-time tracking and app support enhancements. The release signals broader deployment of AI agents.

cards.title: Meta plans cloud business selling AI compute power
cards.body: Meta is developing a cloud infrastructure service to monetize excess AI compute capacity and models. The move positions Meta against AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure.

cards.title: Trump administration drops Anthropic model restrictions
cards.body: The Trump administration lifted restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable AI models. The decision reflects ongoing policy uncertainty around AI model releases.

cards.title: Wayve launches $85M tender offer at $8.5B valuation
cards.body: AI autonomous driving company Wayve initiated an employee tender offer valued at $8.5 billion. The move reflects AI startup strategies for talent retention.

cards.title: OpenClaw AI agent now available on mobile
cards.body: The free open-source agentic program OpenClaw launched on Android and iOS platforms. Mobile availability expands AI agent access to smartphone users.

cards.title: Anthropic launches Claude Science for research
cards.body: Anthropic unveiled Claude Science, a flagship product designed to support scientific research autonomously. The tool parallels Claude Code's software engineering capabilities.

cards.title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
cards.body: Organizations are applying AI to operational frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management. AI enhances structured approaches to organizational efficiency.

cards.title: Teaching AI to run with wind turbines
cards.body: AI is being deployed in physical infrastructure like wind turbines for maintenance and optimization. These applications show AI's impact beyond consumer-facing chatbots.

cards.title: LLMs stuck in groupthink; startup offers solution
cards.body: Language models exhibit predictable patterns when generating responses, creating a 'groupthink' problem in AI. A startup is developing tools to address this AI limitation.

cards.title: Google's AI buildout drove 37% electricity increase
cards.body: Google's artificial intelligence infrastructure expansion caused a 37% surge in electricity consumption during 2025. The energy demands highlight AI's growing infrastructure footprint.
